name: ship-galley
summary: The ship's kitchen — a brick-lined iron stove, swinging pots, a chopping block, every surface strapped or stowed against the roll of the sea.

primary_palette.note: iron brick-lined stove, heart of the galley
cook's work surface with a cleaver buried in it

story_prompts: cook is a retired adventurer and still a dangerous foe
a poisoning has just been discovered — the cook flees or fights
everything is bolted down, the galley has ridden out worse storms
the stove's fire hasn't gone out in twenty years — now it's guttering
anti_patterns: stove hugs a bulkhead, never floats in the middle
don't leave the floor bare — pots, racks, barrels stack tightly
pot-racks overhead (as props), not freestanding in the walk-space
